actual_year1_registrations_units.rationale: Issue 2 names 3,000 domains as the realistic downside scenario at $50/domain; base just below the 5,000 break-even threshold; upper bound captures aggressive defensive-trademark plus speculative early-registration uptake.

actual_delegation_months.rationale: Plan target is 18 months; Risk 1 names 6–12 month delay from sovereign objections, Risk 5 names 3–6 month delay from political backlash; upper bound captures combined cascade.

actual_year1_total_cost_usd.rationale: Plan envelope is $25–100M+; base reflects realistic Builder-strategy Year-1 spend including 5 FTE × 18 months + ICANN fees + outreach; upper bound captures Risk 3 $10–20M overrun scenario.

actual_mars_endpoint_kbps.rationale: Mars relay capacity is highly uncertain in 2027–2028; base just below the 100 kbps target reflects realistic relay-network constraints; downside captures dust-storm-degraded operation.

actual_hsm_capex_usd.rationale: Issue 3 names $5–10M for FIPS 140-2 Level 3 HSM procurement; base just above the $5M floor reflects realistic vendor pricing; downside captures partial procurement or reverted Decision-4 choice.

year1_breakeven_threshold_units.rationale: Issue 2 names 5,000 domains as the break-even floor at $50/domain; spread reflects price-elasticity assumptions — lower break-even at higher prices, higher break-even at lower prices.

mars_mos_kbps.rationale: Plan commits to 100 kbps as the MOS floor; spread reflects how strictly the High-Freshness tier is interpreted — looser at the lower end (Beta Tier), stricter at the upper end.
